tasks.name: Save 3 recent, highly cited cryptocurrency fraud working papers to a new reading list
tasks.description: Find working papers on cryptocurrency fraud published from 2021 onward with at least 50 citations, and save the 3 most recently published ones to a new reading list named 'Crypto Fraud 2024'. Success means exactly 3 papers meeting these criteria are added to that list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the main search bar in the header.
Type "cryptocurrency fraud" into the search field and press Enter to run the search.
In the left sidebar, click the "Publication Type" filter and select the "Working Paper" checkbox.
In the "Year" filter, set the minimum year field to "2021" and apply the filter.
In the "Citations" filter, set the minimum citations to "50" and apply the filter.
Open the "Sort by" dropdown above the results and select "Publication Date (Newest First)".
Click on the title of the first result to open its detail page, then click the "Add to Reading List" button and choose the option to create a new list named "Crypto Fraud 2024".

Use the site breadcrumb or back button to return to the results list, then open the second and third results one by one and, on each detail page, click "Add to Reading List" and select the existing "Crypto Fraud 2024" list.

tasks.name: Select the larger, compact credit card fraud dataset and add it to the dataset cart
tasks.description: Identify two credit card fraud datasets, compare them, and add to the dataset cart the one with more observations while having fewer than 50 variables. Success means exactly one dataset matching these criteria is added to the dataset cart.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Datasets" link in the main navigation menu.
Click the dataset search bar and type "credit card fraud", then press Enter to search.
In the filters panel, click on "Data Type" and select the option labeled "Micro-level transaction data" (or equivalent).
In the "License" filter, select an option that allows research use, such as "Non-commercial research".
Open the "Sort by" dropdown above the dataset results and select "Number of Observations (High to Low)".
Ctrl+click (or use the equivalent method) on the first and second dataset titles in the results list to open each in a separate browser tab.

Switch to the first dataset tab, scroll to the dataset summary section, and locate both the "Number of Observations" and "Number of Variables" fields; note whether the number of variables is under 50 or 50 and above.

Switch to the second dataset tab, find the same "Number of Observations" and "Number of Variables" fields, and determine which of the two datasets has more observations while having fewer than 50 variables.
Remain on or switch to the dataset tab that satisfies "more observations" and "under 50 variables", then click the "Add to Dataset Cart" button on that dataset's page.

tasks.name: Add an APA citation for a Latin America tax evasion article in a high-impact journal to a citation export list
tasks.description: Find an economics journal article on tax evasion in Latin America, published between 2015 and 2022, where the journal impact factor is above 3. Then save its APA citation to a citation export list named 'Tax Evasion Assignment'. Success means one citation matching all criteria is in that export list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage and click on the "Publications" or "Articles" section in the main navigation.
Use the search bar within this section to type "tax evasion Latin America" and press Enter.
In the filters, set the publication year range by entering "2015" as the start year and "2022" as the end year, then apply the filter.
In the "Document Type" filter, select "Journal Article".
Locate the "Journal Impact Factor" filter and set the minimum value to "3.0" (or the closest available option above 3), then apply this filter.
In the results list, click on the title of the first article that appears after the filters are applied to open its detail page.
On the article detail page, click the "Cite" or "Citation" button and choose the "APA" citation style from the style options.
In the citation options, click "Add to Citation Export" (or similar) and create a new export list named "Tax Evasion Assignment".
Confirm the action by clicking the button to save or add the APA-formatted citation to the "Tax Evasion Assignment" export list.

tasks.name: Create an in-site notification alert for new Ponzi scheme working papers
tasks.description: Set up a saved search with an in-site notification alert for Ponzi scheme working papers published from 2010 onward, scheduled to notify you weekly. Success means a saved search named 'Ponzi Schemes 2010+' is created with notification frequency set to weekly.
steps: From the homepage, click on the main search bar and type "Ponzi scheme" then press Enter.
In the search results, apply the "Publication Type" filter by selecting "Working Paper".
In the "Year" filter, set the minimum year to "2010" and apply the filter to restrict results to 2010 and later.
Open the "Sort by" dropdown and choose an option such as "Relevance" to keep the most relevant items at the top.
Locate and click the "Save Search" or "Create Alert" button that appears near the top of the filtered results page.
In the dialog that appears, enter "Ponzi Schemes 2010+" into the saved search name field.
Within the same dialog, find the notification type settings and select an option that specifies in-site notifications (such as "Dashboard notifications" or equivalent).
Set the notification frequency dropdown to "Weekly" and then click the "Save" or "Create" button to finalize the alert.

tasks.name: Follow two money laundering experts with at least 5 publications each
tasks.description: Find and follow two authors whose research focuses on money laundering and who each have at least 5 publications in the database. Success means exactly two such authors are added to your followed authors list.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Authors" or "Researchers" section in the main navigation.
In the author search bar, type "money laundering" and press Enter to search for authors associated with this topic.
In the filters for authors, find and select a topical filter or tag labeled "Money Laundering" (or similar) to narrow down to specialists in this area.
Locate the "Sort by" dropdown for authors and select "Number of Publications (High to Low)".
Click on the name of the first author in the sorted list to open their profile page, then on the profile locate the publications count and ensure it shows 5 or more publications.
On that author’s profile page, click the "Follow" button to start following this author.
Use the breadcrumb or "Back to results" link to return to the filtered author list, then click on the second author in the list whose profile indicates 5 or more publications.
On the second author’s profile page, click the "Follow" button to add this author to your followed authors list.

tasks.name: Create a collection of 4 recent open-access financial statement fraud detection articles
tasks.description: Compile a collection containing 4 English-language, open-access journal articles on financial statement fraud detection, published between 2018 and 2023, each with a full-text download option. Success means a collection named 'FS Fraud Detection 2018–2023' contains exactly 4 such articles.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Publications" or "Articles" section.
Use the search bar to type "financial statement fraud detection" and press Enter.
In the filters panel, set the publication year range by entering "2018" as the start year and "2023" as the end year, then apply the filter.
In the "Access Type" filter, select "Open Access" and apply the filter.
In the "Language" filter, select "English".
In the results list, look for items marked with a full-text download indicator (such as a "PDF" icon) and click on the title of the first such article to open its detail page.
On the article detail page, click the "Add to Collection" button and choose the option to create a new collection named "FS Fraud Detection 2018–2023".

Return to the results list using the breadcrumb or back button and, for the next three articles that show a full-text download indicator and match the filters, open each detail page in turn and click "Add to Collection", selecting the existing "FS Fraud Detection 2018–2023" collection each time.

Optionally sort the results by "Publication Date (Newest First)" before choosing the remaining articles to ensure they fall within the specified 2018–2023 range while still being open access and in English.

tasks.name: Bookmark a retracted LIBOR manipulation article and tag it for case study use
tasks.description: Identify a retracted article related to LIBOR manipulation and bookmark it with a 'case study' tag. Success means one retracted LIBOR manipulation article is bookmarked with the 'case study' tag.
steps: From the homepage, click on the main search bar and type "LIBOR manipulation" then press Enter.
In the filters panel, locate the "Publication Status" or "Integrity" filter and select the "Retracted" status.
Apply the status filter and then open the "Sort by" dropdown to select an option such as "Publication Date (Newest First)" or "Relevance".
Click on the title of the first retracted article in the filtered results list to open its detail page.
On the article detail page, click the "Bookmark" or "Save" button.
In the bookmarking options that appear, locate the tags or labels field and type "case study" as the tag.
Confirm the bookmark by clicking the "Save" or "Add" button so that the retracted LIBOR manipulation article is saved with the "case study" tag.

tasks.name: Add a European financial crime conference with a registration fee under $500 to your planner
tasks.description: Find an upcoming conference on financial crime or fraud in Europe scheduled between September and December 2025, with a registration fee under $500, and add it to your conference planner. Success means exactly one qualifying conference is added to the planner.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Events" or "Conferences" section in the main navigation menu.
In the events search bar, type "financial crime" and press Enter to search.
Use the "Region" or "Location" filter to select "Europe".
Set the event date range filter so that the start date is "2025-09-01" and the end date is "2025-12-31", then apply the filter.
Locate the "Registration Fee" filter and set the maximum fee to "500" (US dollars or the closest equivalent option provided), then apply the filter.
Open the "Sort by" dropdown and select "Start Date (Earliest First)" to see upcoming relevant conferences first.
Click on the title of the first conference in the filtered list to open its detail page.
On the conference detail page, click the "Add to Planner" or "Save to My Events" button to add this event to your conference planner.
